1.description: Debt returns and government surpluses. This figure plots the inflation-and-growth-adjusted log debt returnsr∼t$\tilde{r}_t$and the surplus-to-output ratiost$s_t$. Sample period: 1947 to 2022. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Debt-to-output ratio. The full line is the demeanedlog$\log$D-O ratio. The dashed line is the demeanedlog$\log$D-O ratio, demeaned by subsample means before and after 2007. Sample period: 1947 to 2022.

3.description: Variance decomposition of log debt-to-output ratio. This figure reports the variance decomposition of the log D-O ratiovt$v_t$into components due to future government surpluses∑j=1Tst+j$\sum _{j=1}^T s_{t+j}$, future real growth-adjusted returns (with a minus sign)∑j=1T−r∼t+k$\sum _{j=1}^T -\widetilde{r}_{t+k}$, future log D-O ratiovt+T$v_{t+T}$, and the difference between future surpluses and returns. Sample is annual, 1947 to 2022. We plot one s.e. (dark) and two s.e. (light) confidence intervals. Standard errors are generated from 10,000 simulated samples, each constructed from bootstrapping the joint residuals in a regression of(rt,vt)$(r_t,v_t)$on two lags ofvt$v_t$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Variance decomposition of log debt-to-output ratio after bias correction. This figure reports the variance decomposition of the log D-O ratiovt$v_t$into components due to future government surpluses∑j=1Tst+j$\sum _{j=1}^T s_{t+j}$, future discount rates∑j=1Tr∼t+k$\sum _{j=1}^T \widetilde{r}_{t+k}$, future log D-O ratiovt+T$v_{t+T}$, and the combination of the future government surpluses and discount rates. We generate bias-corrected slope coefficients using the Stambaugh (1999) and Boudoukh, Israel, and Richardson (2022) small-sample bias correction for the OLS coefficients in long-horizon predictability regressions. Sample is annual, 1947 to 2022. We plot one s.e. (dark) and two s.e. (light) CIs. Standard errors are generated from 10,000 simulated samples, each constructed from bootstrapping the joint residuals in a regression of(rt,vt)$(r_t,v_t)$on two lags ofvt$v_t$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Variance decomposition of log debt-to-output ratio with simulation-based bias adjustment. This figure reports the variance decomposition of the log D-O ratiovt$v_t$into components due to future surpluses∑j=1Tst+j$\sum _{j=1}^T s_{t+j}$, future real growth-adjusted returns (with a minus sign)∑j=1T−r∼t+k$\sum _{j=1}^T -\widetilde{r}_{t+k}$, the future log D-O ratiovt+T$v_{t+T}$, and the difference between the future surpluses and returns. The red curve reports the series without bias adjustment, and the blue curve reports the series with bias adjustment based on simulation under the data-generating process given in (6). Sample is annual, 1947 to 2022. We plot one s.e. (dark) and two s.e. (light) CIs. Standard errors are generated by bootstrapping 10,000 draws from the same data-generating process (DGP). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Variance decomposition of log debt-to-output ratio controlling for lags. This figure reports the variance decomposition of the log D-O ratiovt$v_t$into components due to future government surpluses∑j=1Tst+j$\sum _{j=1}^T s_{t+j}$, future discount rates∑j=1Tr∼t+k$\sum _{j=1}^T \widetilde{r}_{t+k}$, the future log D-O ratiovt+T$v_{t+T}$, and the combination of the future government surpluses and discount rates. The red curve reports the series without bias adjustment, and the blue curve reports the series with bias adjustment based on simulation under the data-generating process given in (6). Sample is annual, 1947 to 2022. We plot one s.e. (dark) and two s.e. (light) CIs. Standard errors are generated by bootstrapping 10,000 draws from the same DGP. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: Variance decomposition of log debt-to-output ratio: Longer sample 1842 to 2022. This figure reports the variance decomposition of the log D-O ratiovt$v_t$into components due to future log D-O ratioρTvt+T$\rho ^T v_{t+T}$, and the combination of the future government surpluses and discount rates. Because of data limitations, the D-O ratio is the market value of gross debt including both marketable and nonmarketable debt. We generate bias-corrected slope coefficients using the Stambaugh (1999) and Boudoukh, Israel, and Richardson (2022) small-sample bias correction for the OLS coefficients in long-horizon predictability regressions. The red curve reports the series without bias adjustment, and the blue curve reports the series with bias adjustment based on simulation under the DGP given in (6). Sample is annual, 1842 to 2022. We plot one s.e. (dark) and two s.e. (light) CIs. Standard errors are generated by bootstrapping 10,000 draws from the same DGP. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

8.description: Variance decomposition of log debt-to-output ratio with break. This figure reports the variance decomposition of the transitory component of the log D-O ratiovt−v¯t$v_t-\bar{v}_t$with a lower pre-2007 sample mean (v¯t,t<2007$\bar{v}_t,t<2007$) and a higher post-2007 sample mean (v¯t,t≥2007$\bar{v}_t,t\ge 2007$) into components due to future government surpluses∑j=1Tst+j$\sum _{j=1}^T s_{t+j}$, future discount rates∑j=1Tr∼t+k$\sum _{j=1}^T \widetilde{r}_{t+k}$, future log D-O ratiovt+T−v¯t+T$v_{t+T}-\bar{v}_{t+T}$, and the combination of the future government surpluses and discount rates. Sample is annual, 1947 to 2022. We impose a structural break in the log D-O ratio by shifting its average level before and after 2007. We generate bias-corrected slope coefficients using the Stambaugh (1999) and Boudoukh, Israel, and Richardson (2022) small-sample bias correction for the OLS coefficients in long-horizon predictability regressions. We plot one s.e. (dark) and two s.e. (light) confidence intervals. Standard errors are generated from 10,000 simulated samples, each constructed from bootstrapping the joint residuals in a regression of(rt,vt)$(r_t,v_t)$on two lags ofvt$v_t$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

9.description: Comparing CBO and private-sector surplus forecasts. This figure plots the forecast, made at the start of the year (in January), for the current calendar year surplus-to-GDP ratio. The dashed line is for the Congressional Budget Office (CBO) forecast while the solid line is the mean forecast from Consensus Economics, a data set of private-sector forecasters. Each line combines forecasts for the government surplus, debt service, real GDP growth, and inflation from the respective data sets. Since the Consensus Economics data start in 1994, we use the longest overlapping sample of 1994 to 2021 for this graph.

10.description: CBO projections for debt-to-output and surplus-to-output. This figure plots the five(10)-year U.S. federal government D-O (top panel) and total federal government primary surplus-to-GDP (bottom panel) projections made by the CBO at the start of each year from 1980 to 2022. For the period from 1980 to 1995, the five-year projections are reported. For the period from 1996 to 2022, the 10-year projections are reported. The solid black line is the ex post realized D-O (top panel) and surplus-to-output ratio (bottom panel). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

11.description: CBO projection errors. This figure shows the difference between the annualized cumulative future surpluses∑j=1Tst+jT$\frac{\sum _{j=1}^T s_{t+j}}{T}$using the CBO projection and the annualized cumulative realized future surpluses, wherest+j=syt+j/ev$s_{t+j}=sy_{t+j}/e^v$andv$v$is the average log D-O ratio between 1980 and 2022. The CBO projections go out five years (T=5$T = 5$) from 1980 to 1995 and 10 years (T=10$T = 10$) from 1996 to 2022. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

12.description: Variance decomposition of log debt-to-output ratio: CBO projection 2007 to 2022. This figure shows the variance decomposition of the log D-O ratiovt$v_t$into components due to future log D-O ratioρTvt+T$\rho ^T v_{t+T}$, and the combination of the future government surpluses and discount rates. All estimates are bias-corrected. We generate bias-corrected slope coefficients using the Stambaugh (1999) and Boudoukh, Israel, and Richardson (2022) small-sample bias correction for the OLS coefficients in long-horizon predictability regressions. Sample is annual, 1947 to 2022. From 2007 to 2022, we use CBO projected values for∑j=1Tst+j$\sum _{j=1}^T s_{t+j}$,∑j=1Tr∼t+j$\sum _{j=1}^T \tilde{r}_{t+j}$, andvt+T$v_{t+T}$. Standard errors are generated by bootstrapping 10,000 draws from the joint residuals in a regression of(rt,vt)$(r_t,v_t)$on two lags ofvt$v_t$. We plot one s.e. (dark) and two s.e. (light) CIs for the sample using CBO projected values. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

13.description: Debt-to-output ratio: The role of the Fed and the foreign sector. The solid line plots the D-O ratio. The dashed line plots the domestically and privately held D-O ratio. Data Source: Flow of Funds and CRSP. Sample period: 1947 to 2022.

14.description: Time-varying fiscal conservatism. This figure shows the time-varying coefficientsδts$\delta ^s_{t}$of the regressionst+1=a+δts(vt−v*)+εt+1$s_{t+1} = a + \delta ^s_{t} (v_t - v^{\ast }) + \varepsilon _{t+1}$. The top panel plots the 30-year rolling-window regression coefficients, and the bottom panel plots the estimation result from Bayesian linear regression with time-varying coefficients. Sample period: 1947 to 2022. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

15.description: Log D-O ratio. This figure plots the log D-O ratio series in levels in the top left panel and in first differences in the bottom left panel. The right panels plot sample autocorrelations for these two series.

16.description: Forecasting power of log debt-to-output ratio for actual government surplus. This figure plots regression coefficients associated with the log D-O ratio.Sraw$S^{raw}$is based on the actual government surplus-to-GDP ratio. We generate bias-corrected slope coefficients using the Stambaugh (1999) and Boudoukh, Israel, and Richardson (2022) small-sample bias correction for the OLS coefficients in long-horizon predictability regressions. Sample is annual, 1947 to 2022. We plot one s.e. (dark) and two s.e. (light) confidence intervals. Standard errors are generated from 10,000 simulated samples, each constructed from bootstrapping the joint residuals in a regression of(rt,vt)$(r_t,v_t)$on two lags ofvt$v_t$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

17.description: Variance decomposition of log debt-to-output ratio: Longer sample 1842 to 2022. This figure reports the variance decomposition of the log D-O ratiovt$v_t$into components due to future log D-O ratioρTvt+T$\rho ^T v_{t+T}$, and the combination of the future government surpluses and discount rates. The data for the market value of marketable debt only start in 1916, so D-O ratio is the market value of gross debt including both marketable and nonmarketable debt. We generate bias-corrected slope coefficients using the Stambaugh (1999) and Boudoukh, Israel, and Richardson (2022) small-sample bias correction for the OLS coefficients in long-horizon predictability regressions. Sample is annual, 1842 to 2022. The red curve reports the series without bias adjustment, and the blue curve reports the series with bias adjustment based on simulation under the data-generating process given in (6). We plot one s.e. (dark) and two s.e. (light) CIs. Standard errors are generated by bootstrapping 10,000 draws from the same DGP. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

18.description: Variance decomposition of log debt-to-output ratio: Longer Bohn sample 1916 to 1995. This figure reports the variance decomposition of the log D-O ratiovt$v_t$into components due to future log D-O ratiovt+T$v_{t+T}$and the combination of the future government surpluses and discount rates. We generate bias-corrected slope coefficients using the Stambaugh (1999) and Boudoukh, Israel, and Richardson (2022) small-sample bias correction for the OLS coefficients in long-horizon predictability regressions. Sample is annual, 1916 to 1995. The red curve reports the series without bias adjustment, and the blue curve reports the series with bias adjustment based on simulation under the data-generating process given in (6). We plot one s.e. (dark) and two s.e. (light) CIs. Standard errors are generated by bootstrapping 10,000 draws from the same DGP. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

We generate bias-corrected slope coefficients using the Stambaugh (1999) and Boudoukh, Israel, and Richardson (2022) small-sample bias correction for the OLS coefficients in long-horizon predictability regressions. 21.description: Variance decomposition of log debt-to-output ratio: withρ=exp(−1%)$\rho =\exp (-1\%)$. This figure reports the variance decomposition of the log D-O ratiovt$v_t$into components due to future log D-O ratioρTvt+T$\rho ^T v_{t+T}$, and the combination of the future government surpluses and discount rates. We generate bias-corrected slope coefficients using the Stambaugh (1999) and Boudoukh, Israel, and Richardson (2022) small-sample bias correction for the OLS coefficients in long-horizon predictability regressions. 22.description: Decomposition for CBO projections. The plot shows the decomposition of the log D-O ratiovt$v_t$into components due to CBO-projected (and realized) future government surpluses∑j=1Tst+j$\sum _{j=1}^T s_{t+j}$, future discount rates∑j=1Tr∼t+k$\sum _{j=1}^T \widetilde{r}_{t+k}$, forT=10$T=10$. We also report future real growth∑j=1Tx∼t+k$\sum _{j=1}^T \widetilde{x}_{t+k}$. All numbers are annualized. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

23.description: Variance decomposition of log debt-to-output ratio: CBO projection 1996 to 2022. This figure reports the variance decomposition of the log D-O ratiovt$v_t$into components due to future log D-O ratioρTvt+T$\rho ^T v_{t+T}$, and the combination of the future government surpluses and discount rates. All estimates are bias-corrected. We generate bias-corrected slope coefficients using the Stambaugh (1999) and Boudoukh, Israel, and Richardson (2022) small-sample bias correction for the OLS coefficients in long-horizon predictability regressions. Sample is annual, 1947 to 2022. From 1996 to 2022, we use CBO projected values for∑j=1Tst+j$\sum _{j=1}^T s_{t+j}$,∑j=1Tr∼t+j$\sum _{j=1}^T \tilde{r}_{t+j}$, andvt+T$v_{t+T}$. Standard errors are generated by bootstrapping 10,000 draws from the joint residuals in a regression of(rt,v∼t)$(r_t,\widetilde{v}_t)$on two lags ofv∼t$\widetilde{v}_t$. We plot one s.e. (dark) and two s.e. (light) CIs for the sample using CBO projected values. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

24.description: Forecasting power of log debt-to-output ratio held by the domestic sector. This figure reports regression coefficients associated with the log domestically held D-O ratio. We generate bias-corrected slope coefficients using the Stambaugh (1999) and Boudoukh, Israel, and Richardson (2022) small-sample bias correction for the OLS coefficients in long-horizon predictability regressions. Sample is annual, 1947 to 2022. The red curve reports the series without bias adjustment, and the blue curve reports the series with bias adjustment based on simulation under the data-generating process given in (6). We plot one s.e. (dark) and two s.e. (light) CIs. Standard errors are generated by bootstrapping 10,000 draws from the same DGP. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

25.description: Variance decomposition of log debt-to-output ratio using VAR. This figure reports the variance decomposition of the log D-O ratiovt$v_t$into components due to future government surpluses∑j=1Tst+j$\sum _{j=1}^T s_{t+j}$, future discount rates∑j=1Tr∼t+k$\sum _{j=1}^T \widetilde{r}_{t+k}$, future log D-O ratiovt+T$v_{t+T}$, and the combination of the future government surpluses and discount rates. The red curve reports the results obtained from local projection after bias adjustment, and the blue curve reports the results obtained from the first-order VAR. The sample is annual, 1947 to 2022. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
